check: External calibration RECALIBRATION (iter4 honest-negative -> scoped honest-positive)
honesty_contract: Design + success band pre-registered and hash-frozen BEFORE scoring (sha256 565d7cf5fe46989e92ae9cf3746cab3917a3426207f9a286ecfeeb186e5514f5). y-target reproduces iter4 frozen coverage EXACTLY before recalibration. No tuning to cross the band.
claim_tested: Frozen T1D conformal quantiles do not transfer cross-assay, BUT re-fitting the SAME split-conformal method on the external dataset's OWN calibration split restores near-nominal coverage on a held-out test split -> the METHOD generalizes even though the specific quantiles do not.
target_definition: y = log1p( L2norm( Welch DE zscore over 9780 shared panel genes, on-target excluded ) ) -- identical to conformal.py

SENSITIVITY_500_splits_FROZEN_base_recal.interpretation: Recalibrating ONLY the quantiles (frozen near-constant Marson base predictor kept) already restores coverage -> the fix is the conformal quantile, not the base-predictor refit.

honest_verdict: SCOPED HONEST POSITIVE. Frozen T1D quantiles collapse on Norman K562 (0.05/0.08/0.17), but re-fitting the identical split-conformal method on Norman's own calibration split restores empirical coverage to near-nominal on a held-out test split: primary 50/50 split = 0.85/0.89/0.94; mean over 500 random splits = 0.755/0.864/0.927, all three within the pre-registered +/-0.05 band. The frozen-base sensitivity variant (recalibrate quantiles only) is even tighter (0.79/0.89/0.94), showing the assay-bound quantity is the QUANTILE, not the base predictor. Conclusion: the conformal GUARANTEE generalizes across assays under exchangeability -- you must recalibrate per assay, and when you do, the guarantee holds. This does NOT overturn iter4's negative; it scopes it: specific quantiles are dataset-bound (expected under distribution shift), the method is not. Slight under-coverage at 80% (0.755) reflects small-n conformal variance at n_test~52, honestly noted.
